intro: Psittacoglossum seymourianum, also known as Indian plantain, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Asparagaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Indian plantain include flavonoids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
